title: Know Emergency Numbers
summary: Learn when and how to call for help in an emergency, and keep important numbers and your address where you can find them quickly.
learnerPromise: Know when and how to call for help in an emergency.
whyItMatters: In an urgent situation, every second counts. Having emergency numbers and your address ready helps you get help faster and stay calmer.
realLifeUses: Calling for help when someone is hurt and needs an ambulance
Reporting a fire or smelling smoke in a building
Contacting local police for a safety concern
Knowing who to call for a non-urgent medical question
youWillLearn: Your local emergency number and when to use it
How to explain your location to a dispatcher
The difference between emergency and non-urgent lines
How to store important numbers on your phone or a card
miniChallenge: Look up your local emergency number and write it down with your address.
steps: Look up the emergency number for your country or region.
Write down your full address and keep it somewhere easy to find.
Save the emergency number and a local non-urgent health line in your phone.
Practice saying your address clearly out loud.
Tell a trusted person where you keep the emergency info.
completionCriteria: You know your local emergency number by heart
Your address is written down and easy to find
You saved important numbers in your phone or on a card
commonProblems: You are not sure which number is for your area — search online or ask someone local
You do not know the full address — check a lease, mail, or ask a neighbor
You feel nervous about calling — remember that dispatchers are trained to help you
tips: Keep a written card with emergency numbers on the fridge or near the phone
Some phones allow setting emergency info that shows on the lock screen
Only call emergency numbers for real emergencies — use non-urgent lines for minor questions
